verse_text: The four living creatures, each having six wings, were full of eyes around and within. And they do not rest day or night, saying:

links.context: Ezekiel identifies the four living creatures as cherubim (10:9-16), the guardians of the throne of God ([[EXO.25:10]]-22; 3Kg 6:23-28). Like those of St. John's vision (see [[REV.4:6]]-9), they are seen by the Fathers to typify the Four Evangelists, while their unity in the spirit (vv. 12, 20) illustrates the spread of the gospel.

symbolic.title: Example: The Cherubim of Ezekiel I
